nodes.label: Methodology decision: characterize before optimize
nodes.description: Before Stage 2 optimizes the maintenance loop on top of the Phase-3 cell, run the whole orthogonal scorecard (not trimmed like Phase 2's P2.3/P2.4) because breadth is the cheapest place to catch a latent algorithm bug before it's baked into optimization. Excludes axes needing new architecture (recurrence, conv, large-scale natural data).
nodes.rationale: Earned the framing twice: caught a latent OOM bug in the Bayes-error computation, and refuted the plan's optimistic A7 noise-win expectation.

nodes.label: the three racers: OURS / BP-ceiling / Mono-Forward
nodes.description: OURS = [contrast(InfoNCE,two-mask)+coordination w=2] SCFF bulk + all-tap sleep-consolidated readout (the Phase-3 cell). BP-ceiling = a same-budget genuinely-tuned backprop MLP (lr grid + weight-decay + 2-3 depth/width shapes, chosen config logged -- not strawmanned). Mono-Forward = per-layer local-CE forward-only supervised learner, the 'cheap supervised' honesty check.

nodes.label: backward credit-assignment work (substrate cost metric)
nodes.description: Analytic count = credit-distance (SCFF=0/coord-window, GD=depth) x weights x #backward weight-updates. Explicitly labelled substrate backward work, never 'energy' or 'N x faster' -- a numpy sim cannot measure GPU energy, and the substrate cares about credit-distance, not wall-clock (Spyra 2025's cost-honesty caution).
